“Yes,” Sabrae confirms, ears and tail wobbling as she nods her head. “We can do that now, if you want. There are two classes of implants we have for non-natives, and you can only get one of each. Combat and Utility types. In the future we might be able to fit more but that’s going to be years off, probably... our scientists just aren’t familiar enough with coreward physiology and the last thing we need is a reputation as a butcher shop if we start killing our coreworld subjects. Getting the testing done requires a standard fee of ".concat(Up.toLocaleString()," credits.”")

“Are you a brain surgeon, then?” you ask, half-joking.
“Oh, no,” Sabrae says, smiling. “The procedure is done by the machine. I’m really just here to make sure you pay and give you a warm welcome when you’re done.”
You’re not sure if that’s less or more reassuring.

“Haha!” Iyla laughs, shifting her feet. “No, you’re actually totally right to fear the loan companies. They’re all shell corporations representing the big guns, and they disappear or change names every week.” Her smile fades a little - just for a second, but enough for you to catch it. “The zaibatsu have their fingers in every pie.”

You’re not too certain she’s telling the truth about the origin of her bar - it seems very convenient for a place as hostile and mercenary as Dhaal, as far as you know it.
